item.title: Crewbloom: Sales and Marketing Specialist
item.description:
Headquarters: India
We seek an experienced and dynamic Sales and Marketing Specialist to join our client’s team. The ideal candidate will have a strong background in e-commerce marketing, specifically within the print-on-demand industry, and a proven track record of driving sales and increasing brand visibility. This role requires expertise in digital marketing strategies, social media management, email marketing, SEO, customer relationship management, and influencer marketing, including liaising with agencies representing influencers.Job Responsibilities Develop and implement comprehensive sales and marketing strategies to drive traffic, increase conversions, and maximize revenue for the print-on-demand store. Manage and optimize online advertising campaigns (Google Ads, Facebook Ads, Instagram Ads) to ensure a high return on investment. Create, execute, and monitor email marketing campaigns, including newsletters, promotional offers, and product announcements. Oversee social media accounts, developing content strategies that engage followers, build brand awareness, and drive sales. Conduct market research to identify trends, customer preferences, and competitive landscape to inform marketing strategies. Optimize the Shopify store for SEO, ensuring all product listings, blog posts, and web pages rank highly on search engines. Develop and manage in-store promotions, including discounts, referral programs, and affiliate programs to attract and retain customers. Collaborate with the design team to create visually appealing marketing materials, including banners, ads, and social media posts. Develop and manage influencer marketing campaigns, including liaising with influencer agencies, identifying key influencers, and coordinating product promotions. Analyze sales data and marketing metrics to measure the effectiveness of campaigns and adjust strategies as needed. Develop partnerships and collaborations with influencers, bloggers, and other relevant entities to expand brand reach. Handle customer relationship management, ensuring excellent customer service and promptly addressing any issues or inquiries. Monitor and report on industry trends and advancements, recommending new tools and strategies to stay ahead of the competition. Proven experience as a Sales and Marketing Specialist, preferably in the e-commerce or print-on-demand industry. Strong understanding of digital marketing principles, including SEO, PPC, social media marketing, email marketing, and content marketing. Proficiency with marketing tools and platforms such as Google Analytics, Google Ads, Facebook Ads Manager, Mailchimp, Hootsuite, and similar. Experience with Shopify and its marketing integrations, apps, and tools. Experience in developing and managing in-store promotions, such as discounts, referral programs, and affiliate programs. Excellent communication and interpersonal skills, with the ability to collaborate effectively with team members and external partners. Strong analytical skills, with the ability to interpret data and make data-driven decisions. Experience in influencer marketing, including liaising with influencer agencies and coordinating product promotions. Creative thinking and problem-solving abilities to develop innovative marketing campaigns and solutions. Ability to manage multiple projects simultaneously and meet deadlines in a fast-paced environment. Knowledge of design principles and experience working with graphic designers to create marketing materials. Strong organizational skills and attention to detail. Preferred Qualifications: Bachelor's degree in Marketing, Business, Communications, or a related field. Experience in influencer marketing and managing affiliate programs. Familiarity with additional e-commerce platforms and tools. Knowledge of HTML/CSS for minor website updates and optimizations. Certification in Google Analytics, Google Ads, or other relevant marketing certifications. Experience with software and tools specific to sales and marketing, such as HubSpot, Klaviyo, SEMrush, Ahrefs, and Canva. Minimum Technical and Work Environment Requirements: Internet Connection: Primary internet connection with a minimum speed of 15 Mbps. Backup internet connection with at least 10 Mbps. Backup connection must be capable of supporting work during a power outage. Primary Device: Desktop or laptop equipped with at least: Intel Core i5 (8th generation or newer), Intel Core i3 (10th generation or newer), AMD Ryzen 5, or an equivalent processor. A minimum of 8 GB RAM. Backup Device: Must meet or exceed the performance of an Intel Core i3 processor. Must be functional during power interruptions. Peripherals and Workspace: A functioning webcam. A noise-canceling USB headset. A quiet, dedicated home office space. A smartphone for communication and verification purposes. Join Our Dynamic Team: Experience our fun, inclusive, innovative culture that values your unique contributions and supports your professional growth. Embrace the Opportunities: Seize daily chances to learn, innovate, and excel. Make a real impact in your field. Limitless Career Growth: Unlock a world of possibilities and resources to propel your career forward. Fast-Paced Thrills: Thrive in a high-energy, engaging atmosphere. Embrace challenges and reap stimulating rewards. Flexibility, Your Way: Embrace the freedom to work from home or any location of your choice. Create your ideal work environment. Work-Life Balance at Its Best: Say goodbye to stressful commutes and hello to quality time with loved ones. Achieve a healthy work-life integration to perform at your best.
To apply: https://weworkremotely.com/remote-jobs/crewbloom-sales-and-marketing-specialist

item.title: Spectrum It Recruitment: Senior DevOps Engineer
item.description:
Headquarters: Oxfordshire
A Marketplace on a Mission to the Moon… We are working with an exciting business on a mission to put themselves at the forefront of digital marketplace consumer services. To rival the corporate, and to put the little people - the merchant and the consumer - at the forefront of business success. Having recently been named as Europe's fastest growing ecommerce company by the financial times and having accumulated many accolades from the tech and marketplace industries, the company is ready to scale. Come and join as the company take their marketplace to a global market. Senior DevOps Engineer A crucial role in streamlining software delivery pipelines, enhancing reliability, performance and scalability of systems, and driving continuous improvement across the software lifecycle. There are no passengers in this engineering team, everyone contributes and makes an impact. Your contribution will be significant and will help the ship run smoothly and reach further. What will make you a success in this role? You will need to be experienced, or at least comfortable with, working in a start-up style environment; fast moving, fast thinking, and willing to roll up your sleeves and get stuck in! Senior level technical capability with strong problem solving skills.Proven experience with GCP as either a DevOps Engineer or SRE.A good depth of experience using Terraform and Ansible.A strong knowledge of Kubernetes cluster, building containers, deploying via CI/CD pipelines.Able to think commercially i.e. cost of scaling vs utilisation of existing infrastructure. Beneficial Experience Knowledge of Nginx, PHP, MySQL.Knowledge of NoSQL solutions, RabbitMQ, SOLR, Couchbase.Good understanding of Networking concepts and solutions.Ability to work with, devise, and implement CI/CD pipelines and practices.A solid understanding of networking concepts, technologies, and protocols (TCP/IP, IPSec, HTTPS, DNS). The company have a remote first working practice so this position is available on a remote working basis. If you prefer working in an office with a team of people, they have a fully kitted, modern office based near Bournemouth in Dorset. You're welcome as often as you like. Salary, approx. £75,000 - £90,000 + benefits, remote working, generous benefits Hit apply to upload your CV or contact Tom Rayner at Spectrum IT Recruitment for more info. Spectrum IT Recruitment (South) Limited is acting as an Employment Agency in relation to this vacancy.
To apply: https://weworkremotely.com/remote-jobs/spectrum-it-recruitment-senior-devops-engineer

item.title: Restaurant365: Product Designer
item.description:
Headquarters: Mexico City
Restaurant365 is a SaaS company disrupting the restaurant industry! Our cloud-based platform provides a unique, centralized solution for accounting and back-office operations for restaurants. Restaurant365’s culture is focused on empowering team members to produce top-notch results while elevating their skills. We’re constantly evolving and improving to make sure we are and always will be “Best in Class” ... and we want that for you too!At Restaurant365, we’re revolutionizing the restaurant industry by creating intuitive, user-centric solutions that make managing and growing restaurants easier. As a Product Designer, you will play a pivotal role in shaping the user experience for restaurant professionals, ensuring that our products not only look great but also solve real-world problems. This role is ideal for designers looking to take their skills to the next level in a dynamic, fast-paced environment.  How you'll add value: Design Impactful Experiences: Lead design efforts on small to medium-sized projects, creating thoughtful visual design, interaction design, and high-fidelity prototypes that enhance the user journey.  Collaborate Cross-Functionally: Work closely with product managers, engineers, and other stakeholders to bring your designs to life, ensuring alignment with product goals and business objectives.  Participate in Design Reviews: Present your work to team members and stakeholders, incorporating feedback to iterate and improve designs, always with an eye toward the user’s needs.  Contribute to User-Centered Solutions: Utilize user research, feedback, and data analysis to ensure your designs solve real pain points and elevate the overall product experience.  Enhance Design Consistency: Maintain and contribute to Restaurant365’s design system to ensure cohesive user experiences across all platform components.  Other duties as assigned. What you'll need to be successful in this role: 3+ years of professional experience in product design, UX/UI, or a related field, with a strong portfolio that demonstrates your problem-solving and user-focused design skills.  A keen interest in creating intuitive, user-friendly designs that tackle complex problems with creativity and precision.  Strong expertise in design tools such as Figma, Sketch, or Adobe Creative Suite, with the ability to produce high-quality wireframes, mockups, and interactive prototypes.  Ability to work effectively with cross-functional teams and value the contributions of product managers, engineers, and fellow designers in the design process.  A deep understanding of the importance of user research and usability testing, along with the ability to iterate based on feedback and data.  Comfort working in a fast-paced environment with multiple projects, shifting priorities as necessary to meet business and user needs.  PREFERRED QUALIFICATIONS Prior experience designing for SaaS platforms or B2B products, especially in the restaurant or hospitality industries, is highly desirable.  In-depth understanding of UX/UI principles and best practices to ensure that designs are functional, elegant, and easy to use.  Experience contributing to or maintaining design systems to ensure consistency and efficiency across product lines.  Ability to distill complex user needs and business goals into clean, functional, and aesthetically pleasing design solutions.  DYN365, Inc d/b/a Restaurant365 is an equal opportunity employer.
To apply: https://weworkremotely.com/remote-jobs/restaurant365-product-designer

item.title: Method Grid: Product Manager
item.description:
Headquarters: Bath, Avon
Background to RoleMethod Grid is a scale-up SaaS business with a prestigious client base and serious ambition. Our platform helps organisations connect knowledge, process and delivery - turning playbooks into live, auditable workflows that teams actually use. From capital infrastructure projects to renewable energy, you’ll see the impact of your work in places where assurance, compliance and repeatable quality really matter. As we continually develop the product (including a raft of new AI-enabled capabilities), we’re creating this role so you can help us lead of our product function and help us deliver more value, more often.About YouYou love turning messy, real-world problems into simple, useful outcomes. You get close to customers, listen hard and synthesise what you learn into sharp problem statements and measurable goals. You keep a tidy backlog, make trade-offs clear and write crisp stories and acceptance criteria that teams can act on. You’re at your best collaborating with Design and Engineering - curious, pragmatic, low-ego - and you use data to guide decisions, whether that’s reading funnels and cohorts or pulling a quick SQL query to answer a question. You think about quality early - performance, reliability, security and auditability - and you communicate clearly, keeping people aligned with short, regular updates. Experience in B2B SaaS is important; familiarity with assurance/compliance, governance or reporting is a bonus.The RoleYou’ll work closely with our Chief Product and Technology Officer to agree what success looks like, then run lightweight, continuous discovery with customers and colleagues to keep us focused on what matters. You’ll define simple scope - PRDs, user stories and acceptance criteria - so delivery stays clear and fast. You’ll prioritise the roadmap for impact, effort and risk, keep it honest as things change, and work hand-in-hand with Design and Engineering to ship small, frequent improvements. You’ll instrument analytics, learn from experiments and iterate quickly. Along the way, you’ll champion non-functional needs and build in the controls and audit trails our customers expect. When it’s time to ship, you’ll co-ordinate releases, enable the team with “what’s changed”, and loop feedback straight back into the next iteration.Why Join?Method Grid is an early-stage SaaS platform with an ever-growing list of impressive customers. We’ve secured significant investment both to accelerate the build of our product and to hire a team of passionate experts to deliver an outstanding customer experience.It’s going to be an exciting journey. We believe we have a unique product that will only get better.We celebrate diversity and how it can empower a high-performing team. But the one thing that does unify us is our values. As we grow, we intend to keep our values front-&-centre.We have a great (and growing) team of co-workers who embody the cultural values of:>Team (highly collaborative)Central Rock (client-centricity)Never Settle (never ending pursuit of excellence coupled with humility to recognise we never get there)Egos at the Door (take our work seriously but not ourselves)Fast Craft (ruthless speed when it comes to product development)
To apply: https://weworkremotely.com/remote-jobs/method-grid-product-manager

item.title: Openprovider: Product Marketing Manager
item.description:
Headquarters: India Remote
About the company Openprovider is an ICANN-accredited domain registrar and technology company founded in 2004 in Rotterdam, Netherlands. We are a fully-remote organisation with more than 100 team members spread across 20+ countries. Remote working means no office, no painful commuting, and no stressful traffic - all you need is yourself, a laptop, and a cup of coffee!A diverse and inclusive organisation, we support a healthy work/life balance and are constantly looking for new ways to help the well-being of our people. We support a flexible schedule and are 100% performance and result-oriented. At Openprovider, we're not just a company; we're a mission-driven team dedicated to transforming the domain industry. We believe in providing a trusted digital identity to every business. Our innovative approach is reshaping the industry from a traditional transactional model to a more customer-centric, subscription-based approach. We pay subscriptions for music, newspapers, and movies - why should domains be any different?Come join us on our journey to create a trusted digital world. About the role We are looking for an experienced Product Marketing Manager who will create and deliver compelling narratives that showcase our product's unique value, engaging customers, attracting prospects, and aligning internal teams. You will drive business growth through strategic go-to-market (GTM) plans that capitalise on market opportunities.We are full of ideas and energy. We are always on the lookout for new technology and innovative ways to represent Openprovider’s core values and bring our mission to life. We are relentless in our pursuit to deliver increasingly effective and engaging campaigns, articles, and webinars across all our platforms. Key responsibilitiesRegularly conduct and update market research.Analyse customer feedback and product usage data.Monitor competitor activities and strategies.Create and refine customer segmentation models.Develop messaging frameworks for different customer segments.Position products to highlight unique value propositions and advantages.Produce sales materials, including pitch decks, datasheets, and FAQs.[both internally and externally(for customers)]Provide ongoing support and updates to sales and product teams.Collaborate with product management to define feature priorities.Gather and integrate customer feedback into product development and/or sales enablement.Communicate roadmap updates to internal stakeholders.Evaluate product performance and market alignment.Identify gaps and opportunities for new products or enhancements.Align portfolio management with strategic business objectives.Collaborate with strategic sales to set and adjust pricing.Analyse market pricing trends and customer price sensitivity.Use pricing as a strategic tool in product positioning.Provide customer insights and feedback to the product team.Facilitate customer advisory boards or focus groups.Provide strategic insights to guide long-term business decisions.Publish regular reports or newsletters on market trends and innovations.What we are looking for3+ years of experience as a Product Marketing Manager in Hosting/Domains/SSL or SaaS industry;B2B experience;Experience of working in international teams;Fluent English;BSc or MSc in Marketing or a relevant field;Proven experience in market research, customer feedback usage, and competitor analysis;Affinity in developing customer segmentation models and messaging frameworks;Expertise in positioning products strategically in the market;Ability to work closely with sales and product management teams;Capability to communicate product roadmap updates clearly to stakeholders;Experience in facilitating customer advisory boards and focus groups.Competence in defining and prioritising product features;Expertise in analysing market pricing trends;Skill in using pricing as a strategic tool in product positioning;Proficiency in presenting strategic insights to guide business decisions.What we offer100% remote work (you can work from any location, no need to go to the office);Paid time off and sick leave;An international team and regular online and offline events to stay connected.Internal workshops and knowledge-sharing sessions;Quarterly review and annual salary review;Flexible working hours (We are a result-oriented company);Budget for learning;We are an ISO 27001-certified remote workplace.About us Openprovider is a wholesaler of domains and internet security products. We provide a platform for domain resellers that allows them to easily manage large numbers of domain names for an affordable price. Our people always come first for us - and that includes both our customers and our colleagues. We believe in the power of collaboration and working together to achieve the best results, and we pride ourselves on our flexible work culture and result-oriented approach.We have been fully remote since 2020, and our team has only expanded since then. Our team currently consists of 110+ employees, who are spread across 20+ different countries and 4 different continents. We are proud of our fun and diverse team and regularly connect with each other through online and offline events.
To apply: https://weworkremotely.com/remote-jobs/openprovider-product-marketing-manager

item.title: Cooper Lomaz Recruitment: Senior DevOps Engineer
item.description:

item.title: Coupa Software: Sr. Product Manager
item.description:
Headquarters: Ann Arbor, Michigan, United States
Coupa makes margins multiply through its community-generated AI and industry-leading total spend management platform for businesses large and small. Coupa AI is informed by trillions of dollars of direct and indirect spend data across a global network of 10M+ buyers and suppliers. We empower you with the ability to predict, prescribe, and automate smarter, more profitable business decisions to improve operating margins.Why join Coupa? Pioneering Technology: At Coupa, we're at the forefront of innovation, leveraging the latest technology to empower our customers with greater efficiency and visibility in their spend. Collaborative Culture: We value collaboration and teamwork, and our culture is driven by transparency, openness, and a shared commitment to excellence. Global Impact: Join a company where your work has a global, measurable impact on our clients, the business, and each other. Learn more on Life at Coupa blog and hear from our employees about their experiences working at Coupa. The Impact of a Sr. Product Manager at Coupa:We are seeking an experienced Senior Product Manager to lead the strategy, execution, and evolution of our Clarity Design System while driving AI-powered capabilities that shape the future of our product experiences. This role sits at the intersection of design, technology, and innovation, enabling consistency, scalability, and intelligence across our platform.You’ll partner closely with design, engineering, and AI teams to ensure our design system becomes a robust foundation for delivering delightful, accessible, and efficient user experiences—while also integrating cutting-edge AI components to support our broader AI strategy.What You'll Do: Own the vision and roadmap for our Design System, ensuring it aligns with product and brand strategy.Define and deliver AI-driven enhancements within the design system, such as adaptive components, personalization, and intelligent UI patterns.Partner with cross-functional teams (Design, Engineering, AI, Product) to implement scalable design solutions across multiple products and platforms.Establish governance frameworks, best practices, and contribution models for the Design System.Drive adoption and evangelization of the Design System across internal teams and external partners.Collaborate with AI product leaders to identify opportunities for integrating AI-driven capabilities in core components and workflows.Monitor emerging trends in design systems, AI, and UX to ensure we remain at the forefront of innovation.Define and track success metrics for both design system adoption and AI integration impact.What You Will Bring to Coupa: 5+ years of product management experience, including experience with design systems or design tooling at scale.Strong understanding of UX principles, design tokens, component libraries, and accessibility standards.Experience working with AI/ML technologies and understanding their application in design or product experiences.Proven ability to manage complex roadmaps, prioritize competing demands, and deliver outcomes at scale.Excellent communication and stakeholder management skills—able to influence across design, engineering, and leadership teams.Familiarity with front-end frameworks (React, Angular, etc.), design tools (Figma)Background in component-driven development and design-to-code workflows.Passion for creating consistent, beautiful, and intelligent user experiences.#LI-AA2#LI-RemoteCoupa complies with relevant laws and regulations regarding equal opportunity and offers a welcoming and inclusive work environment. Decisions related to hiring, compensation, training, or evaluating performance are made fairly, and we provide equal employment opportunities to all qualified candidates and employees. Please be advised that inquiries or resumes from recruiters will not be accepted.By submitting your application, you acknowledge that you have read Coupa’s Privacy Policy and understand that Coupa receives/collects your application, including your personal data, for the purposes of managing Coupa's ongoing recruitment and placement activities, including for employment purposes in the event of a successful application and for notification of future job opportunities if you did not succeed the first time. You will find more details about how your application is processed, the purposes of processing, and how long we retain your application in our Privacy Policy.
To apply: https://weworkremotely.com/remote-jobs/coupa-software-sr-product-manager

item.title: Mactores: Full Stack Developer
item.description:
Headquarters: Mumbai, MH
Mactores is a trusted leader among businesses in providing modern data platform solutions. Since 2008, Mactores have been enabling businesses to accelerate their value through automation by providing End-to-End Data Solutions that are automated, agile, and secure. We collaborate with customers to strategize, navigate, and accelerate an ideal path forward with a digital transformation via assessments, migration, or modernization.We are seeking a passionate and detail-oriented Full Stack Developer (Associate) with 2+ years of experience to join our team. In this role, you will build and maintain marketing web pages, develop interactive demos using cutting-edge AI tools (such as v0 and Lovable), and contribute to both front-end and back-end infrastructure. This is a hands-on role where you’ll code daily, collaborate with cross-functional teams, and help shape engaging digital experiences for our customers.What you will doBuild and maintain marketing pages and interactive demos leveraging AI tools (e.g., v0, Lovable).Develop high-quality, testable, and efficient code that can scale to serve a significant number of users.Architect, implement, and drive improvements to front-end and back-end infrastructure.Ensure proper implementation of security and data protection measures.Follow full lifecycle software development practices, including design, coding, testing, deployment, and maintenance.Collaborate with design and marketing teams to deliver seamless, responsive user experiences.Identify opportunities to enhance user engagement through innovative tech solutions.Stay updated on emerging trends in web development and inform senior leadership of new tech ideas, products, and approaches that could improve business growth and customer experiences.Adhere to cloud application development practices and create high-quality, testable, and efficient code that can scale with a significant number of users.What you need for this opportunityBachelor's degree in Computer Science, Computer Engineering, or related field.2+ years of hands-on experience in full-stack development.Proficiency with MongoDB, Express.js, React.js, Node.js, HTML, and CSS.Strong understanding of RESTful APIs and service security best practices.Familiarity with GitHub and collaborative coding practices.Experience in building highly scalable, data-intensive products.Proficiency with OOP languages and solid problem-solving skills.Knowledge of responsive design techniques and mobile-first development.Strong communication and collaboration skills, with the ability to work in a fast-paced startup environment.You are preferred if you haveExperience/ Knowledge working with Ecommerce portals.Unit Testing frameworks and tools (Karma, Mocha, Jasmine, Jest, etc)Life at MactoresWe care about creating a culture that makes a real difference in the lives of every Mactorian. Our 10 Core Leadership Principles that honor Decision-making, Leadership, Collaboration, and Curiosity drive how we work.1. Be one step ahead2. Deliver the best3. Be bold4. Pay attention to the detail5. Enjoy the challenge6. Be curious and take action7. Take leadership8. Own it9. Deliver value10. Be collaborativeWe would like you to read more details about the work culture on https://mactores.com/careers The Path to Joining the Mactores TeamAt Mactores, our recruitment process is structured around three distinct stages:Pre-Employment Assessment: You will be invited to participate in a series of pre-employment evaluations to assess your technical proficiency and suitability for the role.Managerial Interview: The hiring manager will engage with you in multiple discussions, lasting anywhere from 30 minutes to an hour, to assess your technical skills, hands-on experience, leadership potential, and communication abilities.HR Discussion: During this 30-minute session, you'll have the opportunity to discuss the offer and next steps with a member of the HR team.At Mactores, we are committed to providing equal opportunities in all of our employment practices, and we do not discriminate based on race, religion, gender, national origin, age, disability, marital status, military status, genetic information, or any other category protected by federal, state, and local laws. This policy extends to all aspects of the employment relationship, including recruitment, compensation, promotions, transfers, disciplinary action, layoff, training, and social and recreational programs. All employment decisions will be made in compliance with these principles.Note: Please answer as many questions as possible with this application to accelerate the hiring process.
To apply: https://weworkremotely.com/remote-jobs/mactores-full-stack-developer

item.title: Zocks: Senior Product Marketing Manager
item.description:
Headquarters: Remote US
About ZocksZocks is the AI assistant for financial advisors. Our platform saves advisors 10 hours a week by automating their meeting notes, intake forms, client emails and more—so they can focus on what matters most: their clients. We’re backed by top investors, used by thousands of advisors, and growing fast. Now we’re looking for a Senior Product Marketing Manager to help us go even faster.The RoleAs our first Senior Product Marketing Manager, you’ll take the lead on how we position, package, and launch our product. You’ll work closely with marketing, product, sales, and customer success to turn product capabilities into compelling stories that drive adoption, conversion, and growth. You’ll own everything from messaging frameworks and launch plans to sales enablement and competitor intel. This is a strategic and hands-on role—perfect for someone who knows how to dig into the product, talk to users, and ship clear, high-impact work fast.What You’ll DoDevelop product positioning and messaging that’s clear, compelling, and differentiatedOwn and execute product launches—from internal readiness to external campaignsCreate and maintain sales enablement materials (decks, one-pagers, FAQs, etc.)Build and evolve messaging frameworks for features, use cases, and personasPartner with product to influence roadmap and bring new features to marketCollaborate with marketing to develop high-performing content and campaignsConduct competitive research and market analysis to guide positioningTalk to customers regularly to understand their needs and languageWhat We’re Looking For6+ years in product marketing, ideally at a B2B SaaS companyExperience owning product positioning, messaging, and launchesStrong writing skills—you can translate technical features into clear, benefit-driven messagingProven ability to work cross-functionally and lead projects from strategy to executionComfortable interviewing users and using insights to shape messagingScrappy and adaptable—you’re excited to build, test, and iterate quicklyBonus: experience in financial services, advisor tech, or AI-powered productsWhy ZocksCategory-defining product with strong market pull and real user loveHigh-impact role with ownership and autonomy from day oneCompetitive compensation and equityRemote-first culture with flexible hoursHealth, dental, and vision benefitsWant to help define the voice of the most useful AI in financial services?Apply now and let’s build something great together.$170,000 - $190,000 a year
To apply: https://weworkremotely.com/remote-jobs/zocks-senior-product-marketing-manager

item.title: Intec Select Limited: Full Stack Developer
item.description:
Headquarters: City of London, London
Full Stack Developer – £425-475pd PAYE – 12 month contract – Remote Overview:I’m currently partnered with an innovative global technology company who are seeking an experienced Full Stack Developer to join their dynamic tech team. In this role, you’ll be responsible for developing robust, scalable applications from front-end interfaces to back-end systems, contributing to every stage of the software development lifecycle.This is a hands-on development role that requires someone who can work across the entire tech stack from crafting seamless user experiences in ReactJS, to writing robust backend logic in PHP/Hack, and developing automation tools and data pipelines with Python. item.title: Xero: Senior Product Manager
item.description:
Headquarters: Wellington, NZ / Auckland, NZ / Remote, NZ / Hawkes Bay, NZ
Our Purpose At Xero, we’re here to help you supercharge your business. We do this by automating routine tasks, surfacing actionable insights and connecting businesses with the right data, advisors and apps. When that happens, we’re not only making life better for small business, we’ll be building a stronger economy that can change the world.Senior Product Manager - PaymentsAs a Senior Product Manager, you will be responsible for excellence in driving customer and business value through ownership of new and existing products. You will be comfortable working in complex product spaces that require navigating larger degrees of ambiguity and comprehensive stakeholder management. You will manage products end-to-end, from early-stage research and ideation, through to implementation and improvement over time. Your impact will be measured by key commercial and customer success metrics.About the team:Within our Payments Portfolio, you’ll be joining one of  our Get Paid teams to help us deliver the best payment outcomes for our customers and drive Xero’s payments revenue ambitions. This is an exciting time to join the team as we embark on year 1 of a 3 years strategy to transform our payments business.  What we're looking for:5+ years of product management experience, including improving customer workflows and learning through experimentation.A proven track record of delivering products that drive significant customer and business impact.Strong commercial acumen. In payments, we carry financial targets so we need someone that can translate the work into financial outcomes and deliver results.Can get technically deep and engage in detailed engineering design opportunities.Can execute an API-first strategy and support engineering efficiency goals.Have a strong interest in AI and how it can improve outcomes for customers and Xeros.Strong analytical skills, with the ability to interpret complex data and translate insights into actionable strategies.Exceptional communication and stakeholder management skills, comfort with presenting to senior leadership.A growth mindset, with a passion for learning, innovation, and pushing boundaries.What you'll do:Strategise Product Vision: Lead the strategic framing of products within Xero's overall strategy and future objectives.Drive Customer-Centricity: Lead customer engagement, research, and insight collection to embed customer data in decision-making and risk assessment.Problem Identification & Solution Framing: Identify valuable customer problems and comprehensively frame solutions for testing and refinement, even in complex environments.Product Line Strategy & Resourcing: Develop and secure funding and resources for the product line strategy.Oversee Product Delivery & Launch: Lead the development of product roadmaps, release strategies, and ensure effective product launches.Manage Product Risks: Take responsibility for managing non-functional risks (security, privacy, compliance, etc.) and lead trade-off decisions.Monitor & Optimise Performance: Consistently review, monitor, and analyze commercial and customer results, leading iterative improvements to existing products.Guide End-to-End Product Lifecycle: Manage products across their entire lifecycle, from launch to sunset, ensuring continuity and strategic alignment.Develop Product Practice & Team: Contribute to developing and implementing product practices, and train, coach, and recruit team members.Why Xero? Offering very generous paid leave to use however you’d like (plus statutory holidays!), dedicated paid leave to care for your physical and mental wellbeing as well as an Employee Assistance Program to access mental health care for you and your family, free medical insurance, wellbeing and sports programmes, employee resource groups, 26 weeks of paid parental leave for primary caregivers, an Employee Share Plan, beautiful offices, flexible working, career development, and many other benefits that reflect our human value, you’ll do the best work of your life at Xero.
To apply: https://weworkremotely.com/remote-jobs/xero-senior-product-manager-1

item.title: Xero: Senior Product Manager
item.description:

Our Purpose At Xero, we’re here to help you supercharge your business. We do this by automating routine tasks, surfacing actionable insights and connecting businesses with the right data, advisors and apps. When that happens, we’re not only making life better for small business, we’ll be building a stronger economy that can change the world.Senior Product Manager - PaymentsAs a Senior Product Manager, you will be responsible for excellence in driving customer and business value through ownership of new and existing products. You will manage products end-to-end, from early-stage research and ideation, through to implementation and improvement over time. and resources for the product line strategy.Oversee Product Delivery & Launch: Lead the development of product roadmaps, release strategies, and ensure effective product launches.Manage Product Risks: Health insurance, life insurance, and income protection. item.title: Fun.xyz: Technical Product Manager
item.description:
Headquarters: New York, NY
About usAt Fun.xyz, we are pioneering the future of decentralized technology, creating groundbreaking blockchain solutions and web3 applications that engage and empower users worldwide.Our mission is to simplify and supercharge the way people interact with blockchain technology, building products that are as intuitive as they are powerful. From seamless payment solutions to innovative dApps, we’re focused on creating tools that enable users and businesses to unlock the full potential of decentralization.We’re a dynamic team of engineers, designers, and innovators driven by a shared passion for crypto and blockchain technology. At Fun.xyz, we don’t just build products — we’re shaping the future of the web.About the roleAs a Technical Product Manager at Fun.xyz, you will be at the forefront of building cutting-edge blockchain products that redefine user experiences in the decentralized web. You'll collaborate with cross-functional teams to deliver seamless, scalable, and secure solutions, bridging the gap between technical complexity and user-friendly design.This role offers a unique opportunity to work on highly impactful projects, including the development of the highest-converting crypto checkout in the industry. You will shape product strategies, drive execution, and ensure that our solutions empower users and businesses alike.If you're a results-driven leader with a deep understanding of blockchain technology and an entrepreneurial mindset, this role is your chance to make a tangible impact on the evolution of web3.QualificationsYou have a Bachelor's degree in Computer Sciences, engineering or a related field7+ years in Product management and strategy, bonus if a few of those years being in CryptoCrypto-forward experience, including familiarity with onchain activity such as interacting with Ethereum addresses, using ENS, and engaging with dApps or blockchain-based servicesConsistent track record of leading ambitious and ambiguous projectsObsession with product quality and an eye for designExperience in highly-cross functional environmentsExcellent analytical, problem-solving, and decision-making skillsStrong written and verbal communication skillsTechnical experience (Previously a SWE, Protocol engineer, Data analyst, etc.)An entrepreneurial mindset with the ability to succeed in a fast-paced environmentResponsibilitiesHelp Fun.xyz build out the highest converting Checkout product in the industryDefine the strategy and roadmap for engineering, attaining maximum alignment with business values and goalsDeeply understand Fun.xyz’s diverse client and user base, and their nuanced business modelsOwn all facets of a product area from crafting the vision and strategy to executingDive into technologies spanning a mix of off-chain & completely on-chain protocolsPartner closely with engineering, design, data, customer support, legal, and operations teamsMake trade-offs across multiple complex dimensionsLeverage data and analytics to inform strategy and measure successMake multitudes of micro-decisions everyday that will impact the businessWhat sets you apartYou are extremely organizedYou are hyper detail-orientedYou take pride and set a high bar for your work, and will not hesitate to hold others to the same barYou have demonstrated experience influencing stakeholders (partners, executives, engineering, customers) in order to achieve resultsYou have mentored other product managers and/or engineers in the pastYou are a big Do-er and base your work around executionYou are not afraid to roll up your sleeves and dive into the trenches with the team, be it coding, talking to customers, plumbing data, writing documentation, preparing decks, etc.You have a track record of excellence (from school, hobbies, side projects, etc)

item.title: Tenth Revolution Group: Product Manager - fully remote
item.description:
Headquarters: London
A software company which provides a data routing and optimisation platform are looking to recruit a Product Manager to join their rapidly growing scale-up. As a Product Manager you'll play a pivotal role in shaping the future of the platform, working at the intersection of customer needs, business strategy and technology. You will lead discovery efforts to identify opportunities, deliver meaningful solutions to market and continuously measure success to drive improvement and growth. You'll be responsible for informing and executing product strategy and roadmap, championing the voice of the customer and collaborating across engineering, sales, marketing and customer success to ensure successful outcomes. Key Responsibilities Assist in the definition and communication of product vision, strategy and roadmapLead discovery work by conducting weekly customer interviews and synthesising insights into actionable deliverablesTranslate problems into outcomes, shaping product opportunities and prioritising for impactOwn delivery from definition through to go-to-market - write clear specs, validate solutions with users and partner closely with engineeringMeasure the impact of product releases against defined success metrics and iterate to achieve the desired resultsCollaborate cross-functionally with Sales, Customer Success, Marketing and Engineering to deliver valuable product experiencesWork closely with data and market research to inform prioritisation and roadmap decisionsDefine, track, and report on KPIsCommunicate progress, outcomes and strategy to internal and external stakeholders regularlyFoster a customer-centric culture by championing feedback loops from customers, internal users and the market. The successful candidate will have 5 years' experience as a Product Manager in a fast-paced B2B SaaS environment, ideally within marketing tech or data platforms, working as a sole Product Manager or a dedicated Product Manager to a small development team. They are looking for someone with a track record of shipping successful products and owning the full product lifecycle, as well as strong experience working in cross-functional teams including engineers, designers, data analysts and GTM teams.
To apply: https://weworkremotely.com/remote-jobs/tenth-revolution-group-product-manager-fully-remote

item.title: Exact Sourcing Ltd: Customer Support Specialist
item.description:
Headquarters: Cambridge, Cambridgeshire
Our client a fast-growing company providing a Saas solution for Accounting Teams. They are looking for a Customer Support Specialist with an accounting background to help the users of their software. You will handle support tickets create help content and work with tools like Gainsight PX to support customers. It may be you are working in accounts at the moment, with an interest or experience in supporting customers, and an interest in Technology solutions. Key Responsibilities for the Customer Support Specialist: Respond to customer inquiries via chat, email, and ticketing systemsTroubleshoot and escalate product issuesCreate and update help articles and tutorialsAnalyse user behaviour and support Customer Success using GainsightCollect feedback to help improve the product and user experience Requirements for the Customer Support Specialist: 2+ years in customer support, customer success, or educational contentStrong communication, problem-solving, and organizational skillsExperience with Microsoft 365/SharePointAccounting background or experience with accounting software/workpapersBonus: SaaS experience, content performance tracking, or UX knowledge What We Offer for the Customer Support Specialist : Competitive pay and benefitsFully remote workA chance to shape customer experience and content strategySupportive, collaborative team making an impact in the accounting industry By applying for this Customer Support Specialist role, you are agreeing to your CV being held on our database, stored confidentially and securely. Your CV details will be used and kept only to provide recruitment services from us. We will only send your details to clients once discussed with you and your data will not be shared with any other third party. You can ask for the deletion or correction of your data, or to opt out of this service at any point by informing Exact Sourcing. We very much look forward to hearing from you and will be reviewing all applications over the next 2 to 3 weeks. If you are selected, we will be in contact and if not, we will be keeping your details on file for any future vacancies. Exact Sourcing is an equal opportunities employer.
